content: agent2: implementing 'Add Token Pattern Tracking' feature - adding return_repeated_pattern param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch core.py's encode method only. Let me know if you're also touching encode().

agent2: my plan for encode() - adding keyword-only param 'return_repeated_pattern: bool = False' at the end of the signature (after disallowed_special), return type becomes list[int] | tuple[list[int], dict]. Implementation only touches the return statement, wraps existing try/except result in 'tokens' var then optionally computes pair counts. Should be additive and shouldn't conflict with your transformers param as long as we both append keyword-only params at the end. I'll edit now - ping me if you already started editing this method so we can merge without clobbering.

agent2: checked my working copy of tiktoken/core.py - it only has my return_repeated_pattern change, no transformers param present. Looks like we're in separate working directories/environments (each submitting our own patch.txt), so no actual file clobbering risk on disk. I've finished my change (return_repeated_pattern param added after disallowed_special, before the closing paren) and am finalizing my patch now.
